definitions: An enclosed or partially enclosed space that has restricted entry and exit, is not designed for continuous human occupancy, and in which hazardous atmospheres or other hazards may exist.

A space that is large enough and so configured that an employee can bodily enter and perform assigned work; has limited or restricted means for entry or exit; and is not designed for continuous employee occupancy.
examples: Working in a confined space requires special training.
OSHA regulations apply to confined space entry.
Ventilation is crucial when entering a confined space.
